title: Fermented Silky Portuguese Dates Casserole
description: A paleo-friendly, fermented meal-prep dish made with stoned dates, cure, and a silky texture.
instructions: Soak the date pits in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
In a small bowl, mix together the sea salt, black pepper, and almond flour.
Add the melted coconut oil to the bowl and stir until well combined.
In a separate bowl, mix together the soaked date pits, coconut milk, and apple cider vinegar.
Add the coconut milk mixture to the bowl with the salt mixture and stir until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x9 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Cover the dish with a lid or plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
After 24 hours, remove the dish from the refrigerator and top with the raw honey (if using).
Return the dish to the refrigerator for another 24 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve chilled, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
